title: T-003 — site_user_access table + 1-primary constraint + RLS (§9.2, V-MS-05, V-MS-06)
prompt:
# T-003 — site_user_access + 1-primary unique index

PRD: docs/prd/14-MULTI-SITE-PRD.md §9.2, §11.2 V-MS-05/V-MS-06
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Introduce the `site_user_access` table backing D-MS-11 cross-site RBAC. Enforce V-MS-06 (exactly one is_primary=true per user per org) at DB level via unique partial index. RLS scopes rows to caller's org via app.current_org_id().

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/0042_site_user_access.sql`:
   - CREATE TABLE site_user_access per §9.2 DDL: id, org_id, site_id (FK sites), user_id (FK auth.users), role, is_primary DEFAULT false, active DEFAULT true, granted_by, granted_at, revoked_at, UNIQUE(org_id, site_id, user_id, role).
   - ENABLE + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.
   - Policy `sua_org_context` USING (org_id = app.current_org_id()) WITH CHECK (org_id = app.current_org_id()). Note: app.current_site_id() is intentionally NOT used here — site_user_access is org-scoped master data; per §6.4 site-level filtering is not applicable to this table.
   - CREATE INDEX idx_sua_user ON site_user_access(user_id) WHERE active.
   - CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_sua_primary ON site_user_access(org_id, user_id) WHERE is_primary AND active.
2. Drizzle schema `packages/db/src/schema/site-user-access.ts`.
3. Tests: V-MS-06 unique-violation when two primaries inserted for same user; V-MS-05 cross-org isolation; FK constraints enforced.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0042_site_user_access.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/site-user-access.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/site-user-access.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
<!-- AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F14a 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost) -->
1. Given migration 0042 is applied, when \d site_user_access inspected, then 10 cols + 2 indexes (idx_sua_user, idx_sua_primary) + RLS+FORCE RLS present.
2. Given a user has is_primary=true row at site_X, when a second is_primary=true row at site_Y for the same (org_id, user_id) is inserted, then DB raises unique_violation (V-MS-06).
3. Given user A in org_A has site_user_access at site_X, when app_user is in session under org_B, then SELECT returns zero rows (V-MS-05 RLS).
4. Given site_id FK references sites table, when sites row is set active=false but site_user_access rows still exist, then INSERT to site_user_access for that site succeeds at FK level (active=false is not a FK constraint — handled separately by V-MS-21 in T-024).
5. Given pg_policies for site_user_access, when inspected, then policy references app.current_org_id() function (no raw GUC reads).

## Test strategy
- RED: assert V-MS-06 unique-violation, V-MS-05 RLS isolation, pg_policies grep before migration.
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test:integration -- site-user-access'.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use TRIGGER to enforce V-MS-06 — partial unique index is the canonical mechanism per PRD §9.2.
- Do not add ON DELETE CASCADE on site_id FK (decommission flow is gated, never cascades).
- Do not read raw GUCs; use app.current_org_id().
- Do not couple role column to a hardcoded enum — roles come from 02-SET roles_catalog reference table.
- Do not skip FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY.

acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0042 is applied, when \\d site_user_access inspected, then 10 cols + 2 indexes (idx_sua_user, idx_sua_primary) + RLS+FORCE RLS present.
Given a user has is_primary=true row at site_X, when a second is_primary=true row at site_Y for the same (org_id, user_id) is inserted, then DB raises unique_violation (V-MS-06).

Given user A in org_A has site_user_access at site_X, when app_user is in session under org_B, then SELECT returns zero rows (V-MS-05 RLS); AND given pg_policies for site_user_access when inspected then policy references app.current_org_id() with no raw GUC reads.

Given site_id FK references sites table, when sites row is set active=false but site_user_access rows still exist, then INSERT to site_user_access for that site succeeds at FK level — active=false handled by V-MS-21 in T-024.

Do not add ON DELETE CASCADE on site_id FK (decommission is gated, never cascades).
